title: Convex affine interpolation preserves an absolute-value bound

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the interpolation parameter lies in [0,1] and both endpoint values are bounded in absolute value by M, then the affine interpolant is also bounded by M.
informal.expected_conclusion: The convex interpolant preserves the absolute-value bound.

declarations.name: affineInterp
declarations.statement: def affineInterp (theta y0 y1 : Real) : Real := (1 - theta) * y0 + theta * y1

declarations.name: affineInterp_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem affineInterp_abs_le (theta y0 y1 M : Real) (htheta0 : 0 <= theta) (htheta1 : theta <= 1) (hy0 : |y0| <= M) (hy1 : |y1| <= M) : |affineInterp theta y0 y1| <= M

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `affineInterp_abs_le` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem affineInterp_abs_le (theta y0 y1 M : Real) (htheta0 : 0 <= theta) (htheta1 : theta <= 1) (hy0 : |y0| <= M) (hy1 : |y1| <= M) : |affineInterp theta y0 y1| <= M
Use abs_add_le, abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg, and mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left.
